title: pterolobium borneense
intro: Pterolobium borneense, also known as Borneo stinking rose, is a plant species native to the tropical regions of Borneo and Southeast Asia, characterized by its pink to red flowers and strong, unpleasant odor. The medicinal properties of Pterolobium borneense are attributed to its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antibacterial properties, which have been used to treat various health conditions. The plant is believed to have several health benefits, including reducing fever, alleviating respiratory issues, and acting as an antiseptic to treat wounds and skin infections. In traditional medicine, Pterolobium borneense is often prepared as a tea or infused in oil to be used as a topical application for its medicinal properties.
